review_text: Nice quite park close to beach and lake
Very nice place to stay by the water also got boat ramp so launching yo boat or ski is easy

Do not attend this park if your caravan is over 25 feet!
Management will say they can fit you, but the park is NOT facilitated for large vans . Slab is smaller then annex snd unable to open annex window flats as sites so small , the caravan is to close for us to open annex window flaps etc.

Beautiful place, very helpful and friendly staff. My wife fell over in front of a staff member and he came straight over with a first aid kit to bandage her leg.

Excellent marriage saver service where they park your caravan. Not a huge park so this is very welcome.

It's clean and almost everyone who stays here seems friendly. Only downside was the games room where none of the machines worked and the pool table was missing a ball. So 4.9 stars. Everything else is amazing. Picture is taken from my site and it's not even 'waterfront'.

Waterfront means you can cast off from your site. Amazing

I have stayed at this caravan park twice and thought I'd give it one more go considering the last time I was there the staff were rude with no carefactor sure enough I went back and nothing had changed||People in general that are staying in these caravan parks have no respect or regard when it comes to tent sites people kept running through our tent site and the caravan that reversed into our tent took no responsibility was up till 3:00 a.m. no security also these ppl camped over our tent site as well no respect or regard when I told the recepriion  she said that's how it is just like when they were running through the tent site  when I told the lady at the desk she said it was my problem to deal with it told me to tell the ppl to stop that is not my job||I'm one of the many Australians that don't have a home and can still see selfish greed on both sides of the fence whether staff or othe campers there it's like this caravan park tolerates bad behaviour for money and it's very obvious||Don't feel sorry for businesses that go broke because of greed I'm starting to see the big picture
Great place to stop know caravan
Sehr ordentlicher Caravan Park mit direktem Zugang zum Lake. Sanitäranlagen sind ausreichend vorhanden und sehr sauber. Der Ocean ist in ein paar Minuten zu Fuß zu erreichen. Sehr nett.
Super helpful and kind staff, helped me out when i was missing anything with my set up!!

We stayed in our Motorhome one row back from the water view sites. Still able to enjoy the views.
Park allowed dogs to stay when it’s not peak season.  Amenities are clean and in good condition.  Park itself has a lovely feel.
Lovely staff - always accommodating
Love this place,  perfect location and the staff go over and above to make sure you enjoy your stay

Our stay was average, limited activities for kids no pool just the lake out the front, quite loud at night with hooning cars over the bridge was unable to pay for late check out despite being told on check in to just come back Saturday to arrange
What a great van park, vans are parked with the owners tractor to perfection, and the views are amazing across Lake Macquarie.

I took my little motorhome for a drive. I ended up down at the entrance and across the river from the entrance is a little Caravan Park. I give them a ring to see if I was able to get in and they suggest they had some vacancies. They said come at 12 o’clock and they book me in. They were so nice and they looked after me really well. they had a little tractor and the man on it drove it so well the caravans coming they unhook and the man with the tractor put them in on their site. It was so well set up. There was no worries about arguing who to go left or who to go right the tractor just come picked it up put it in that spot. No worries at all it made it so relaxing. I felt like when I got there I should’ve just handed the tractor driver the keys so he could park my motorhome but all is good. The toilets and all the functions around are so clean. The atmosphere in the park is so good and the views are terrific even if you’re not parked on the river you still got terrific use around you. It was so nice staying at the tourist park. I think all the stuff for looking after us so well I highly recommend coming and visiting the tourist Park from Michael.
Always a great stay
Great location,  beautiful views from every site
Great location and easy access for caravan
Great customer service, Zoe was a super helpful 10/10
Great place spent many holidays here

The water view is amazing but the cabins are ridiculously small for the ridiculously expensive cost.|The rooms are so small the bedroom door doesn’t close as the bed is in the way. |We had to take turns trying to get into the room.  It would be impossible for a family (2 adults & 3 kids) to stay in these cabins.  I thought the bedside tables had been removed so asked for them to be returned & was told the rooms are too small to accommodate bedside tables.  So water, phones ect will have to go on the floor.|I now know why they don’t put photos of the interior of the cabins on their website.  They are giving a false impression of the cabins & hiding the truth about the cabin size.|Also no hairdryers.  We arrived in the pouring rain & got soaked unpacking the car.  I enquired about hairdryers & was told none are supplied, I have never had this at any holiday park I have been too.  The lovely lady at reception managed to find one for me (I think it might have been her own)|My suggestion is Dunleith put photos of the interior of the cabins on their website so people can make an informed decision if they want to pay extreme costs for a miniature cabin.  I have stayed at many, many cabins across Australia and these are dismal. Extremely disappointing

We love this park.  We have stayed overnight as a stop over and had extended stays before.  Gorgeous views on the waterfront.  Friendly staff.  Very clean ammenities and a short walk to eateries etc.  definitely a go to place for us

This is an older caravan park however it is well maintained. It isn’t top level and doesn’t profess to be. They great staff park your van very efficiently and pull out as well. The sites can be tight so this service was appreciated. Amenities Re older but clean and it’s right on the lake close to the mouth. Walk across the bridge to The Entrance. Recommend if you aren’t looking for 5 star and reasonable price
Absolutely fabulous place to stay
Location, Location, Location... the view is amazing 👏 🤩

Never actually got to arrive at park. Spoke to a lady who answered phone quite aggressively and was in quite a mood. She didn’t want to speak to us and wanted to abandoned the call asap…………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………….
It was good but the rooms were small

Great spot.
Nice park.
Excellent for fishing
Beautiful views.
Great spot not far from  town. Clean park and  amenities. Had a great spot  for our van  right next to the water.. would stay here again
Beautiful little spot. The view from our cabin was great, nice clean park. Lovely place to come n relax. Go for a paddle in the water.
Stayed in our caravan on a waterfront site.  Park was well maintained. Amenities were spotless. Staff very friendly. Would recommend this park.
Great location. Handy that they use their tractor to park your caravan. Right on the water and near the beach.

Very  well  maintained, presented, located caravan  park on  The  Entrance  channel,  most  sites  with  awning  concrete  slabs,  amenities  modern  and  clean, great  location  if you  love  fishing/swimming.  A short  walk/drive  across  Bridge into  town.  Rates  reasonable  for  water  front  park,  sufficient  camp  kitchen, great  Cafe outside  of  park.
Great van park. Awesome location. And they park your van for you.

Chill spot we stayed on a powered drive through site. They have a mini truck thing to pull the vans into the waterfront sites so anyone could potentially tally fit which I thought was cool. The fishing is good here. Amenities were clean enough. No kids playground or anything but my kids enjoyed scootering around fishing so really what else do you need. Close enough to cafes. Taffy's across the bridge was nice and a good spot for food. Kids enjoyed the water area over there too.
Very quiet.very friendly staff and travellers who keep coming back..staying here at moment even though the weather isn't good still good atmosphere.good for fishing and kayaking.
No frills park. Good hot showers. Outdoor staff work hard all day. They park your van as sites very tight. Dog friendly. Only downside, No pool
Great park, great location, very clean amenities, staff busy keeping park up to scratch all day. They actually put your van on site with a tractor.

During a very stressful time when my puppy was in intensive care at SASH Tuggerah and I needed a site for my van to be close by, the staff were very accommodating and helpful. Lovely spot, nice amenities only drawback for me was I would have liked to see  more tree shaded sites.

Love the experience here, friendly staff members and very friendly neighbours it's nice to meet new people, perfect tourist park with everything useful external (laundry, showers and toilets) small walking distance, very peaceful and quiet
We got there late, but the staff was super nice. The lady at the counter was so sweet. The place was beautiful and the facilities were nice. Too bad we could only stay for one night.

Caravan traveller here. Stayed at Dunleith Tourist Park a few months ago & been meaning to write review since then.
I don't write many reviews so it takes something special to prompt me to do so.
Firstly the staff. You would think that the staff were actually the owners of the park. The way in which they maintain and care for the facilities and surrounds is first class. well done.
There is an option to have "Valet" parking of your van to your site with the small tractor operated by a competent team member.
Admin staff are experienced and professional in their role also.
Secondly the location. Beautiful waterfront location, superb sundown views. I know that this is "weather" dependant, we were fortunate to have lovely weather conditions for our stay.
Thirdly Pet Friendly. Other dog owners complied with standard Park rules regarding on leash & waste removal so thanks to them also.
Lastly, A little more information.
Dunleith T.P. is a small caravan park so think about booking ahead.
Not really a Children's holiday location, there's no swimming pool or bouncing pillow. There's a playground just outside the security gate and the beach within walking distance.
Anyway, Thanks Dunlieth T.P. You are on our list for a return visit.
Friendly staff and a nice place to stay.
Great van park close to town and on the water

"We booked a power site last minute for five nights and inquired about extending for two more nights at check-in. The staff assured us it would be easy, and it truly was. From arrival to departure, we felt very welcome. They even offered to help park our van with a tractor and assisted in reattaching it to our vehicle. The location was perfect, close to restaurants and shops. We met a friendly couple with their granddaughter, and to top it off, we were pleasantly surprised by a fireworks display right outside our site. Overall, it was a fantastic stay!"

Stayed recently for 3 days. The parks location is beautiful- right on the estuary near the beach. The park is not big but is well set out and very neat and tidy. The facilities although a bit dated are very clean and it is the first park I’ve been to where all 4 washing machines and 4 dryers were in working order. The staff are lovely. I would happily recommend this park for a stay.
Love Dunleith park.Always very friendly and just a great place to be.
Great park on the water, plenty of places to walk

Had waterfront site which suits the kids fishing, freindly staff, we arrived unhooked the van they came & moved it to the site with a tractor , no fuss. Short walk over bridge to town, Pub, Shopping, Definitely be going back
Very nice spot I recommed this place

I called up to book in for a powered caravan site on the weekend of a music festival. I am a family with five adults and was told I need to book 3 seperate sites as only two adults were allowed per site. Although this is an absolutely ridiculous policy that was not the biggest issue. The lady on the phone was so incredibly rude and was not helpful AT ALL. This is not the first time I have spoken to this lady and she was very rude last time as well. I have also sent multiple email enquiring with no reply. I purely would never go to this holiday park due to this awful service I was given.

We were about to book our mid week winter stay until we found out the price. Seriously, we love the park and its staff but unfortunately the prices are out of reach. $58 a night Dunleith and $40 at other parks some with ocean frontage. Sadly we can't afford to come back.
Spent one night here.  Would have loved to stay longer.  Everything was in perfect order.  Peaceful.  Lots of space.  Would come again.

Booked on Friday afternoon for 2 nights waterfront villa was told by Lissa villa 3 over the phone arrived around 8.30pm collect keys go to villa 3 keys did not open the door to that villa called the park manager this little guy had a attitude saying that it was never villa 3 and then accused me of raising my voice and that he would just refund me and I can leave seemed like he wanted to throw his fists and argue which I did not raise my voice at all I went up to the office he was straight on the defence with every excuse under the sun. I know what I was told when I made the booking the guy was trying to intimdate and went on to say again ill just refund you and leave they said if I wanted villa 3 I would have to pay a cleaning fee to go into villa 3 what a money grabbing establishment they were in the wrong 1000% and wanted me to pay a cleaning fee??? After going to this place for years I WILL NEVER RECOMMEND THIS PLACE TO ANYBODY Rude lying intimidating staff also the hot water ran out when having a shower within 5 minutes zero people skills no customer focus just rude poor disgusting service and a hero staff member that wanted to argue and threaten when they were in the wrong no effort from the little man to apologise and correct the error.DO NOT VISIT THIS OVER PRICED NON CUSTOMER FOCUSED HOLE MINUS 10 RATING POOR DISGUSTING SERVICE AND AN INTIMIDATING MICRO MAN MANAGER SHAME ON YOU BIG THUMBS DOWN FOR THIS PLACE.
We stayed for a night at an unpowered site. Super quiet, great location and amenities were very clean.
Lady at reception needs an Attitude adjustment.....quite rude and grumpy which isn't very welcoming when you have been driving all day. Clean showers and toilets. Nice sites. Good location.
Great place we found by chance. Powered site right on the water front. How awesome. Pet friendly

Stayed in a Waterfront Villa. |I was totally shocked at just how tiny these villas are!|I cannot imagine a family of 2 adults and 3 kids staying in this small area. The triple bunks had the bottom bunk located directly on the floor!! | Only a double bed in a room that is so small you have to walk sidewards to move around the bed. No side tables (it is too small for one on each side but surely they could supply at least one). I had to bring in a chair so I had somewhere to put things next to the bed. The bed is blocking the door from shutting unless you push it fully across to the other wall.|Bathroom is almost too small to dry self, let alone dress self in there. |The 2 seater lounge had lots of sand between the cushions.|Cooking area in the kitchen has only 2 hotplates and a microwave. There is an electric frying pan in cupboard - family meals would be hard to organise in this space so be prepared to eat out. ||No BBQ on the Villa verandah - which is usually provided. ||Also, a reminder SMS sent in the morning of departure informing use to be out by 9.30am - not 10am as is usual at every other van place we have stayed. I can understand that during high peak season but not in low season.
Great location and reasonable rates in off season. Easy walk across the bridge to shops, cafe, playground. There is a public park right next door for the kids. Would come back.

Enjoyed our 2 night stay in our camper trailer. Lovely friendly staff. Great amenities. Nearly needed a seat belt in the shower as the water pressure was fantastic. Close to main town area over bridge. Within walking distance to marine wildlife reserve and oven beach. So many pelicans it was a sight to watch them. Just don't count on the internet but they are trying to fix the problem.
Great place to relax and park up for a few days. Very nice and clean showers and toilets
Great staff & position

My favourite location at The Entrance and I have been coming here since I was a kid with my parents in the late 70’s.
The waterfront cabins are decent but the location trumps everything.
Very well managed tourist park & not far from the main shops , just a stroll across the bridge .
Nice flat grounds for cycling or drag your kayak a few metres from the car & you’re ready to traverse the beautiful Tuggerah Lakes . The onsite games room is brilliant & doubles up as a local museum with plenty of history to be seen .

** I stayed here at waterfront Villa #4 for a couple of days last week as a treat on the way home from a couple of weeks at Forster.
The tourist park is very well managed in all aspects and I met some awesome folks from Galston staying next door at #5 , really nice to have a feed & great chat .
The Entrance looked fantastic and I really enjoyed the stay & location .
Villas can do with an upgrade & a steam clean in the near future but good incentive to get outside & enjoy the outdoors.
Lovely spot on the water. Staff service very good.

We stayed overnight in our Hiace van and had a wonderful experience. We stayed at  Dunleith tourist park for the first time in over 20 years. Service was super friendly, location is excellent. Amenities very clean and they’ve thought of everything with great camp kitchen . Quick trip up from Sydney for a getaway and super relaxing . We’ll be back soon.
I come he this time every year and love coming he its a great place to come for a holiday
Small caravan sites and very close near neighbours. Amenities nice though the floor tiles are very slippery and dangerous. Small park but close to shops and beach.

We regularly visit Dunlieth in our van for short getaways. The Managers are wonderful people, as are all the staff. Professionally run, clean and friendly with reasonable rates. And the sites are fabulous. The views are fantastic. The other side of town, The Entrance, just far enough to get to for dinner or groceries.
Can't rate this park highly enough. Love it!

We came here for camping, only us and one other couple were staying for the tents, the park was busy for the camper vans not for camping  but still the management put our booking right next to the one couple staying for the weekend.  As we have a young child and our 2 years old wake up at 6 30 - 7 am as young kid they usually go to bed by 8pm and wakes up 6 to 7 am. And the next door tent couple shouted at us and our child  in the morning around 7am told us " Excuse us,  they are still sleeping"  It's not a great capming experience for family with young kids. The children shower is dirty with a lot of insects and there are not much to do for the young kids. Apart from the first front row the camper van row/ villas facing the waters are nice. The rest of camping spaces are very jamed next to each others. The park doesn't have much relaxing vibe. It's expensive for camping consider the space and the amenities of what park can offer in our opinions.

I booked a camp site, later changed to a cabin and then I read reviews 🙄 after seeing a few not so good reviews my partner and I were pleasantly surprised with what we arrived too. Cabin is lovely, location is lovely, great night sleep in comfy bed, and we are planning to bring the family back. Thanks to the lovely ladies at front desk for great customer service too.
Nice fishing place
Outstanding location and only a short walk to town. Very neat and tidy, a credit to the owners, cleaners and groundsmen.

Another great stay at Dunleith tourist Park. The office staff and 'tractor' staff go out of their way to make sure your stay is relaxing and enjoyable. Location is fantastic and has everything you need. WE WILL BE BACK. I LOVE Dunleith. Michael..

Terrible. Booked a waterfront cabin on the water. Is this 1990 still? Room very outdated. I’ve seen cleaner prison bunks in silverwater jail than the 3 bunks they have in the second room, full of graffiti and chewing gum stuck underneath with 20mm vinyl mattresses. Main room is a double bed and that’s it.

Private side access near the campers with a gate to a park for the kids and beach.

Save your $$$ and book on the other side of the entrance
Long weekend is 3 days off, why is the minimum of staying 4 nights??
Great place to stay and short walk across the bridge into town.
Lovely park in a great location.We were right on the river and had a great view.We can recommend

Stayed in the caravan part with amazing view of the water but plastic rubbish and ciggie butts was on all the campsites! We arrived at ours and plastic and ciggies from the last person was everywhere and I look around and the same thing on all sites. So close to the water it’s a shame.. not the owners fault as they’re not the ones making the mess but it’s their responsibility to have somebody cleaning up the grounds. Otherwise a very pleasant stay and close to the town.
Cabins in need of updating. Similar priced cabins at other locations have dishwasher, oven, even washing machine, for example.
Great place to stay

A lovely little park.
Love that you can have a tractor park your van 🙂
You have a lovely inlet that you can try your luck fishing.
Well worth a visit.
Lovely park with good fishing spots on the channel

Fantastic location. Fish straight out the back of your van or tent. Vsunset views to town. Cross the bridge to restaurants and the beach just a small walk. Sites are on the expensive side, but best location

Enjoyed our stay at the Dunleith caravan park
Especially the VIP  treatment we got having our caravan towed to our waterfront site .
We will be going there again

5 stars for this gem. We stayed 4 days on a waterfront site. Sensational views and the tractor even parks your van. Amenities were clean and staff were friendly. Fishing right from your site was a bonus.

Awesome place to experience, we stayed right on the waterfront sites. Was amazing for catching fish the kids had a great time catching them right at the van.  The park seems to have loads of upgrades and the service is excellent, they even park your caravan with the tractor for you.  There is a playground located right next to the park but would have loved a small playground in the park for kids :-) thanks again was really great.
